reports.title: Coordinated disclosure inbox
reports.subtitle: Incoming vulnerability reports from the public security page. Triage, investigate, and respond without losing the audit trail.

settings.title: Public security page
settings.description: Publish a public URL where researchers can reach your security team and submit reports. Works out of the box with security.txt conventions.
settings.policyPlaceholder: How you handle reports, your commitment to coordinated disclosure, safe-harbour statement, etc.
settings.slugTaken: That slug is already in use

slug.title: URL slug
slug.body: Short identifier for your public security page URL (lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens). The page will live at /security/<slug>. Pick something short and guessable — researchers often try /security/acme before looking for a security.txt.
slug.ref: CRA Article 13(4) — manufacturers must provide a point of contact for cybersecurity matters. A stable, discoverable URL satisfies this obligation and doubles as the Policy link in security.txt (RFC 9116).

contact.title: Security contact email
contact.body: Dedicated mailbox or alias for vulnerability reports (e.g. security@acme.com). Must be monitored during business hours at a minimum. Published on your security page and baked into the downloadable security.txt Contact field.
contact.ref: CRA Article 13(4) — the contact point must be kept available throughout the product's support period and be able to receive reports of vulnerabilities.

policy.title: Disclosure policy
policy.body: Plain-English statement describing how you handle incoming reports: response times, scope, legal safe harbour for good-faith researchers, and how you coordinate disclosure. Published verbatim on the public page — set expectations before a researcher hits submit.
policy.ref: CRA Annex I Part II(1) — manufacturers must have a coordinated vulnerability disclosure policy. This field is where you publish it.

enablePublic.title: Publish publicly
enablePublic.body: Master switch — when on, the /security/<slug> page is reachable by anyone on the internet and accepts anonymous submissions. When off, the page 404s and the intake is closed. Turn off during setup, turn on once your triage process is ready.
enablePublic.ref: CRA Annex I Part II(1) + Article 11 — the CVD policy and intake must be operational before the product is placed on the market. Leaving this off after launch is non-compliant.

settings.planRequired: Publishing the public security page requires the Business plan.

empty.title: No reports yet
empty.description: When someone submits a vulnerability through your public page, it'll land here for triage.
empty.noProductDescription: Vulnerability reports are tied to your products. Add your first product so researchers and your team have something to report against.

row.notesPlaceholder: What you checked, what you found, next steps. Not visible to the reporter.
